title: Aregelia marmorata
taxonomy: Aregelia marmorata is classified within the family Zygophyllaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the jujube family. This family consists of approximately 170 species, mostly found in arid and semi-arid regions around the world. Aregelia marmorata is a shrub-like plant native to Africa, specifically in the arid and semi-arid regions. The plant's classification is further detailed at the genus and species levels within the broader family Zygophyllaceae. The exact classification of Aregelia marmorata may vary depending on the specific botanical source or database consulted.
uses: Aregelia marmorata is a plant with limited research, but it's said to have potential medicinal uses, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. constituents: Aregelia marmorata, also known as the Western Desert Rose, is a plant species native to Australia.
